data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Visually appealing interfaces are perceived as more usable.
data.technical_spec: Design systems with consistent use of color theory and typography.

data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: Time to acquire a target is a function of the distance and size of the target.
data.technical_spec: Ensure clickable elements are easily accessible and large enough.

data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: Simpler user interfaces reduce decision time.
data.technical_spec: Minimize choices in the navigation to decrease cognitive load.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: Users prefer interfaces to work similarly to familiar ones.
data.technical_spec: Leverage established design patterns for user familiarity.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Limits of human memory should be considered when structuring information.
data.technical_spec: Chunk information into groups of 5–9 to aid retention.
